You are implementing one commit in a Rust project at /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1. Make the code changes and validate them. DO NOT commit, DO NOT push, DO NOT touch version numbers or notes files — only the source/test/README changes described. Report back what you changed.

# Goal
Make the `bot-session` scalar knobs `--result-lines` and `--col-width` honor the config-override hierarchy CLI > workspace `.vc-config.toml` > user config > built-in, exactly like `[bot-session].items` already does. Today they are plain clap defaults with no config layer.

# Background (already true in the code)
- `src/bot_session.rs`: `--result-lines` default is `RESULT_LINE_CAP` (=10, const near top); `--col-width` default is `COL_WIDTH` (=68). Both args currently use `default_value_t = <CONST>` and are plain `usize`.
- `BotSessionParams` (around line 346-357) stores `result_lines: usize` and `col_width: usize`.
- `TryFrom<&BotSessionArgs> for BotSessionParams` copies them straight over.
- `bot_session(ctx, params)` (around line 428) dispatches views: `View::Raw => raw_view(params)`, `View::Fields{unknown_only,per_line} => fields_view(params, unknown_only, per_line)`, `View::Conversation => {}` then does item resolution + `render(...)`.
- `fields_view(params, unknown_only, per_line)` (around line 525) uses `params.col_width` at the aggregated `info!("  {:<width$} ...", width = params.col_width)` site and passes `params.col_width` to `per_line_view(...)`.
- `per_line_view(..., col_width: usize)` already takes a `col_width` param (added recently).
- `render(&t, &items, params.result_lines, ...)` is called in the Conversation branch and uses `result_lines` for the tool-result cap.
- `workspace_items()` (around line 766-776) reads `bot-session.items` from the workspace `.vc-config.toml` via `crate::toml_simple::toml_load` (returns `HashMap<String,String>` — ALL VALUES ARE STRINGS). It's consumed at line ~465-468 for item resolution.
- `resolve_items(&params.toggles, config_items)` layers CLI toggles > workspace/user config > builtin. Mirror this layering for the scalars.
- `src/config.rs`: `UserConfig` struct (lines 78-100) derives Default; has `bot_session_items: Option<String>` set in `load_from` (~line 159) via `map.get("bot-session.items").cloned()`. There are UserConfig struct-literal sites in tests around lines 618 and 758 (and possibly others — grep for `bot_session_items:` to find every construction site).

# Changes

## 1. src/config.rs
- Add two fields to `UserConfig`: `bot_session_result_lines: Option<usize>` and `bot_session_col_width: Option<usize>` (with doc comments matching the style of the existing `bot_session_items` field doc).
- In `load_from`, parse the two new keys from `map`. Values are strings, so parse to `usize`; a PRESENT-but-unparseable value must be a hard error (consistent with the "malformed file → fatal" behavior). Absent key → `None`. Write a small helper, e.g.:
  ```rust
  fn parse_usize_key(map: &std::collections::HashMap<String, String>, key: &str)
      -> Result<Option<usize>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
      match map.get(key) {
          None => Ok(None),
          Some(s) => s.parse::<usize>().map(Some).map_err(|e|
              format!("config key {key:?}: invalid usize {s:?}: {e}").into()),
      }
  }
  ```
  and set the two fields via `parse_usize_key(&map, "bot-session.result-lines")?` / `"bot-session.col-width"`.
- Update EVERY `UserConfig { ... }` struct-literal construction site (grep `bot_session_items:`) to include the two new fields (`bot_session_result_lines: None, bot_session_col_width: None` for the existing ones).
- Add a `#[cfg(test)]` unit test: a config file with `[bot-session]\nresult-lines = 3\ncol-width = 40` parses to `Some(3)`/`Some(40)`; and a bad value (`col-width = "abc"`) makes `load_from` return `Err`.

## 2. src/bot_session.rs
- Change the `--result-lines` and `--col-width` clap args to `Option<usize>` (DROP `default_value_t`). Update their doc comments to note the default value and the config key, e.g. col-width: "First-column ... width [default: 68; or [bot-session].col-width]".
- Change `BotSessionParams.result_lines` and `.col_width` to `Option<usize>` (RAW, unresolved). Update the `TryFrom` impl accordingly (just pass the `Option`s through).
- Replace `workspace_items()` with `workspace_bot_session()` returning a small struct:
  ```rust
  struct WorkspaceBotSession { items: Option<String>, result_lines: Option<usize>, col_width: Option<usize> }
  ```
  It reads the workspace `.vc-config.toml` once (same guard: no workspace / no file → all `None`) and parses `bot-session.result-lines` / `bot-session.col-width` to `usize`, erroring on a bad value (reuse the same parse-usize approach — you may duplicate a tiny local parser or factor a shared helper; keep it simple).
- In `bot_session(ctx, params)`: near the top (before the view `match`), call `workspace_bot_session()?` once and resolve both scalars:
  ```rust
  let col_width = params.col_width.or(ws.col_width).or(ctx.user_config.bot_session_col_width).unwrap_or(COL_WIDTH);
  let result_lines = params.result_lines.or(ws.result_lines).or(ctx.user_config.bot_session_result_lines).unwrap_or(RESULT_LINE_CAP);
  ```
  (Note: result_lines resolves against bot_session_result_lines, col_width against bot_session_col_width — do not cross them.)
- Pass the resolved `col_width` into `fields_view` (add a `col_width: usize` parameter; use it at the aggregated `info!` site and pass it to `per_line_view` INSTEAD of `params.col_width`).
- In the Conversation branch, use the already-resolved `ws.items` for item resolution (replace the old `workspace_items()` call) and pass the resolved `result_lines` into `render(...)` instead of `params.result_lines`.
- Make sure raw_view still works (it needs neither scalar).

## 3. README.md
- In the bot-session section, note that `--result-lines` and `--col-width` now resolve through the same CLI > workspace `.vc-config.toml` > user config > built-in hierarchy as `[bot-session].items`, with config keys `[bot-session].result-lines` and `[bot-session].col-width`. Keep it brief and match the existing prose style.

## 4. tests/cli_bot_session.rs
- Look at existing tests `cli_bot_session_workspace_items` and `cli_bot_session_config_items` for how they write a `.vc-config.toml` / user config into the fixture tempdir and run the CLI. Add tests that:
  - a workspace `.vc-config.toml` with `[bot-session]\ncol-width = 20` makes `--per-line` pad to 20 (assert `format!("  {:<20} {:<9}", "type", "str")` appears), and a CLI `--col-width 12` OVERRIDES it (pads to 12).
  - similarly a workspace `result-lines = 2` caps a tool result to 2 lines (mirror `cli_bot_session_result_lines`), overridden by `--result-lines`.
  Follow whatever mechanism those existing tests use to point the CLI at a workspace/user config (env var, cwd, or a flag).

# Validate (run all, must pass)
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
If clippy or tests fail, fix and re-run until green. Note: the project lints `unwrap_used`/`expect_used` as warnings (→ errors under -D warnings), so avoid `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` in non-test code; if genuinely needed, add `#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]` + a `// OK: <reason>` comment and mention it in your report.

Report: the exact files/functions you changed, the final resolution code, how the workspace-config tests point at the config, and confirmation that fmt/clippy/test are all green.

1	//! The `bot-session` subcommand: display a Claude Code session
2	//! transcript (`.jsonl`) as a readable conversation.
3	//!
4	//! Output is a set of *items* — headers, user, assistant, tool,
5	//! thinking, results, meta, summary — each toggled by `--<item>` /
6	//! `--no-<item>` flags (last one wins), with `--all` / `--none` as
7	//! bulk bases. The default set (headers, user, assistant, tool,
8	//! summary) can be replaced by the user config's
9	//! `[bot-session].items` list; CLI flags then adjust the resolved
10	//! set. Bookkeeping entry types are never rendered.
11	//! A trailing summary line always reports what was hidden or
12	//! skipped. Malformed lines (e.g. a live session's truncated
13	//! last line) warn to stderr and never fail the run.
14	
15	use std::path::PathBuf;
16	
17	use clap::Args;
18	use log::{debug, info, warn};
19	use serde_json::Value;
20	
21	use crate::context::Context;
22	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
23	use crate::transcript::{self, ContentBlock, EntryKind, FileTranscript};
24	
25	/// Default max lines of one tool result shown under `--results`.
26	const RESULT_LINE_CAP: usize = 10;
27	
28	/// Default first-column (dotted-path) width in the `--fields` /
29	/// `--unknown` / `--per-line` views.
30	///
31	/// - 68 aligns the type column for ~99% of observed key paths —
32	///   every structural key except a long tail of
33	///   `snapshot.trackedFileBackups.<absolute path>.*` keys, whose
34	///   embedded absolute paths can be arbitrarily long and so are
35	///   left to overflow.
36	/// - Override with `--col-width`.
37	const COL_WIDTH: usize = 68;
38	
39	/// Max chars of a tool-use one-liner gist.
40	const GIST_CHAR_CAP: usize = 100;
41	
42	/// Clap-derived args for `bot-session`.
43	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
44	pub struct BotSessionArgs {
45	    /// Session transcript .jsonl file to display
46	    #[arg(value_name = "FILE")]
47	    pub file: PathBuf,
48	
49	    /// List the trailing summary line [default: on]
50	    #[arg(
51	        long,
52	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
53	        overrides_with = "no_summary"
54	    )]
55	    pub summary: bool,
56	    /// Do not list the trailing summary line
57	    #[arg(
58	        long,
59	        hide = true,
60	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
61	        overrides_with = "summary"
62	    )]
63	    pub no_summary: bool,
64	
65	    /// List turn headers (=== role time ===) [default: on]
66	    #[arg(
67	        long,
68	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
69	        overrides_with = "no_headers"
70	    )]
71	    pub headers: bool,
72	    /// Do not list turn headers
73	    #[arg(
74	        long,
75	        hide = true,
76	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
77	        overrides_with = "headers"
78	    )]
79	    pub no_headers: bool,
80	
81	    /// List typed user prompts [default: on]
82	    #[arg(
83	        long,
84	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
85	        overrides_with = "no_user"
86	    )]
87	    pub user: bool,
88	    /// Do not list typed user prompts
89	    #[arg(
90	        long,
91	        hide = true,
92	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
93	        overrides_with = "user"
94	    )]
95	    pub no_user: bool,
96	
97	    /// List assistant text [default: on]
98	    #[arg(
99	        long,
100	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
101	        overrides_with = "no_assistant"
102	    )]
103	    pub assistant: bool,
104	    /// Do not list assistant text
105	    #[arg(
106	        long,
107	        hide = true,
108	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
109	        overrides_with = "assistant"
110	    )]
111	    pub no_assistant: bool,
112	
113	    /// List [tool] call one-liners [default: on]
114	    #[arg(
115	        long,
116	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
117	        overrides_with = "no_tool"
118	    )]
119	    pub tool: bool,
120	    /// Do not list [tool] call one-liners
121	    #[arg(
122	        long,
123	        hide = true,
124	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
125	        overrides_with = "tool"
126	    )]
127	    pub no_tool: bool,
128	
129	    /// List thinking blocks [default: off]
130	    #[arg(
131	        long,
132	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
133	        overrides_with = "no_thinking"

135	    pub thinking: bool,

137	    #[arg(
138	        long,
139	        hide = true,
140	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
141	        overrides_with = "thinking"
142	    )]
143	    pub no_thinking: bool,
144	
145	    /// List tool results ([result] lines, truncated) under the
146	    /// [tool] calls that produced them [default: off]
147	    #[arg(
148	        long,
149	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
150	        overrides_with = "no_results"
151	    )]
152	    pub results: bool,
153	    /// Do not list tool results
154	    #[arg(
155	        long,
156	        hide = true,
157	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
158	        overrides_with = "results"
159	    )]
160	    pub no_results: bool,
161	
162	    /// List meta/system/sidechain entries [default: off]
163	    #[arg(
164	        long,
165	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
166	        overrides_with = "no_meta"
167	    )]
168	    pub meta: bool,
169	    /// Do not list meta/system/sidechain entries
170	    #[arg(
171	        long,
172	        hide = true,
173	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
174	        overrides_with = "meta"
175	    )]
176	    pub no_meta: bool,
177	
178	    /// Start from every item on (then subtract with --no-<item>)
179	    #[arg(
180	        long,
181	        alias = "no-none",
182	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
183	        overrides_with = "none"
184	    )]
185	    pub all: bool,
186	    /// Start from no items (then add with --<item>)
187	    #[arg(
188	        long,
189	        alias = "no-all",
190	        help_heading = "Items listed (--no-<item> to disable)",
191	        overrides_with = "all"
192	    )]
193	    pub none: bool,
194	
195	    /// Limit output to a slice of the file's source JSONL lines
196	    /// — the same unit in every view (Note: Index is 0-based):
197	    ///   N    — first N lines (0 = summary only)
198	    ///   -N   — last N lines
199	    ///   I,C  — C lines starting at Index I
200	    ///   I,-C — C lines ending at Index I
201	    /// The conversation view renders the entries in the slice,
202	    /// with elision markers at cut points; the summary always
203	    /// prints.
204	    #[arg(
205	        long,
206	        value_name = "SPEC",
207	        allow_hyphen_values = true,
208	        verbatim_doc_comment,
209	        help_heading = "Output range"
210	    )]
211	    pub lines: Option<String>,
212	
213	    /// Max lines shown per tool result (0 = unlimited)
214	    #[arg(
215	        long,
216	        value_name = "N",
217	        default_value_t = RESULT_LINE_CAP,
218	        help_heading = "Output range"
219	    )]
220	    pub result_lines: usize,
221	
222	    /// First-column (dotted-path) width in the --fields /
223	    /// --unknown / --per-line views; longer paths overflow
224	    #[arg(
225	        long,
226	        value_name = "N",
227	        default_value_t = COL_WIDTH,
228	        help_heading = "Alternate views"
229	    )]
230	    pub col_width: usize,
231	
232	    /// Field inventory instead of the conversation: every dotted
233	    /// path observed per entry type, with count, value kinds, and
234	    /// short samples
235	    #[arg(long, help_heading = "Alternate views", conflicts_with = "raw")]
236	    pub fields: bool,
237	
238	    /// Like --fields but only paths the typed layer does not
239	    /// consume — the unmodeled / new surface
240	    #[arg(long, help_heading = "Alternate views", conflicts_with = "raw")]
241	    pub unknown: bool,
242	
243	    /// Pretty-printed source lines instead of the conversation
244	    /// (unparseable lines pass through verbatim)
245	    #[arg(long, help_heading = "Alternate views")]
246	    pub raw: bool,
247	
248	    /// With --fields/--unknown (implies --fields): list each
249	    /// selected source line's fields separately instead of
250	    /// aggregating across the file
251	    #[arg(long, help_heading = "Alternate views", conflicts_with = "raw")]
252	    pub per_line: bool,
253	}
254	
255	/// Which view the invocation renders.
256	pub enum View {
257	    /// The default conversation rendering.
258	    Conversation,
259	    /// Field inventory; `unknown_only` filters to unmodeled paths,
260	    /// `per_line` lists each source line separately.
261	    Fields {
262	        /// Show only paths not consumed by the typed layer.
263	        unknown_only: bool,
264	        /// Group by source line instead of aggregating.
265	        per_line: bool,
266	    },
267	    /// Pretty-printed source lines.
268	    Raw,
269	}
270	
271	/// A parsed `--lines` slice spec (see `parse_lines_spec`).
272	pub enum LinesSpec {
273	    /// `N` / `-N`: first N (positive) or last N (negative) lines.
274	    Single(i64),
275	    /// `I,C`: 0-based index I with forward (positive) or
276	    /// backward (negative) count C.
277	    Pair(i64, i64),
278	}
279	
280	/// The set of output items the renderer emits.
281	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]
282	pub struct ItemSet {
283	    /// Turn headers (`=== role time ===`).
284	    pub headers: bool,
285	    /// Typed user prompts.
286	    pub user: bool,
287	    /// Assistant text.
288	    pub assistant: bool,
289	    /// `[tool]` call one-liners.
290	    pub tool: bool,
291	    /// Thinking blocks.
292	    pub thinking: bool,
293	    /// `[result]` lines.
294	    pub results: bool,
295	    /// Meta/system/sidechain entries.
296	    pub meta: bool,
297	    /// The trailing summary line.
298	    pub summary: bool,
299	}
300	
301	impl ItemSet {
302	    /// Every item off.
303	    pub const NONE: ItemSet = ItemSet {
304	        headers: false,
305	        user: false,
306	        assistant: false,
307	        tool: false,
308	        thinking: false,
309	        results: false,
310	        meta: false,
311	        summary: false,
312	    };
313	    /// Every item on.
314	    pub const ALL: ItemSet = ItemSet {
315	        headers: true,
316	        user: true,
317	        assistant: true,
318	        tool: true,
319	        thinking: true,
320	        results: true,
321	        meta: true,
322	        summary: true,
323	    };
324	    /// Built-in default view: the conversation essentials.
325	    pub const BUILTIN: ItemSet = ItemSet {
326	        headers: true,
327	        user: true,
328	        assistant: true,

446	/// Run the `bot-session` subcommand: parse the transcript and
447	/// print the conversation view plus the trailing summary line.
448	///
449	/// The item set resolves git-style, most specific wins: CLI
450	/// toggles > workspace `.vc-config.toml` > user config >
451	/// built-in default (`--all`/`--none` are CLI-level bases).
452	pub fn bot_session(
453	    ctx: &Context,
454	    params: &BotSessionParams,
455	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
456	    debug!("bot-session: enter");
457	    match params.view {
458	        View::Raw => return raw_view(params),
459	        View::Fields {
460	            unknown_only,
461	            per_line,
462	        } => return fields_view(params, unknown_only, per_line),
463	        View::Conversation => {}
464	    }
465	    let workspace_items = workspace_items()?;
466	    let config_items = workspace_items
467	        .as_deref()
468	        .or(ctx.user_config.bot_session_items.as_deref());
469	    let items = resolve_items(&params.toggles, config_items)?;
470	    let text = std::fs::read_to_string(&params.file)
471	        .map_err(|e| format!("cannot read {}: {e}", params.file.display()))?;
472	    let total = text.lines().count();
473	    let range = params.lines.as_ref().map(|spec| line_bounds(spec, total));
474	    let (start, end) = range.unwrap_or((0, total));
475	    let t = transcript::parse_str(&text);
476	    for (line_no, err) in &t.malformed {
477	        if *line_no > start && *line_no <= end {
478	            warn!("bot-session: line {line_no}: {err}");
479	        }
480	    }
481	    let (lines, stats) = render(&t, &items, params.result_lines, start, end, total);
482	    for line in &lines {
483	        info!("{line}");
484	    }
485	    if items.summary {
486	        let malformed = t
487	            .malformed
488	            .iter()
489	            .filter(|(n, _)| *n > start && *n <= end)
490	            .count();
491	        info!("");
492	        info!(
493	            "{}",
494	            summary_line(&stats, malformed, range.map(|(s, e)| (e - s, total)))
495	        );
496	    }
497	    debug!("bot-session: exit");
498	    Ok(())
499	}

501	/// Render the `--raw` view: pretty-printed source lines.
502	///
503	/// - `--lines` selects *source JSONL lines* (1-based file lines
504	///   map to 0-based Index), matching what jq/editors see.
505	/// - Parseable lines pretty-print; anything else (malformed,
506	///   truncated) passes through verbatim.
507	/// - No summary, no elision markers — the output is the data.
508	fn raw_view(params: &BotSessionParams) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
509	    let text = std::fs::read_to_string(&params.file)
510	        .map_err(|e| format!("cannot read {}: {e}", params.file.display()))?;
511	    let all: Vec<&str> = text.lines().collect();
512	    let (start, end) = match &params.lines {
513	        Some(spec) => line_bounds(spec, all.len()),
514	        None => (0, all.len()),
515	    };
516	    for line in &all[start..end] {
517	        match serde_json::from_str::<Value>(line) {
518	            Ok(v) => match serde_json::to_string_pretty(&v) {
519	                Ok(pretty) => info!("{pretty}"),
520	                Err(_) => info!("{line}"),
521	            },
522	            Err(_) => info!("{line}"),
523	        }
524	    }
525	    Ok(())
526	}

528	/// One path's aggregate in the `--fields` inventory.
529	#[derive(Default)]
530	struct FieldAgg {
531	    /// How many leaves were observed at this path.
532	    count: usize,
533	    /// Value kinds seen (str / num / bool / null / empty-obj /
534	    /// empty-arr).
535	    kinds: std::collections::BTreeSet<&'static str>,
536	    /// Up to `SAMPLE_CAP` distinct short sample values.
537	    samples: Vec<String>,
538	}
539	
540	/// Max distinct samples kept per path.
541	const SAMPLE_CAP: usize = 3;
542	
543	/// Max chars of one sample value.
544	const SAMPLE_CHAR_CAP: usize = 36;
545	
546	/// Render the `--fields` inventory: every dotted path per entry
547	/// type with count, value kinds, and samples; `unknown_only`
548	/// filters to paths the typed layer does not consume.
549	fn fields_view(
550	    params: &BotSessionParams,
551	    unknown_only: bool,
552	    per_line: bool,
553	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
554	    let text = std::fs::read_to_string(&params.file)
555	        .map_err(|e| format!("cannot read {}: {e}", params.file.display()))?;
556	    let total = text.lines().count();
557	    // --lines selects *source JSONL lines* here, like --raw.
558	    let (start, end) = match &params.lines {
559	        Some(spec) => line_bounds(spec, total),
560	        None => (0, total),
561	    };
562	    let in_range = |line_no: usize| line_no > start && line_no <= end;
563	    let t = transcript::parse_str(&text);
564	    for (line_no, err) in &t.malformed {
565	        if in_range(*line_no) {
566	            warn!("bot-session: line {line_no}: {err}");
567	        }
568	    }
569	    if per_line {
570	        return per_line_view(
571	            &t,
572	            unknown_only,
573	            start,
574	            end,
575	            total,
576	            params.lines.is_some(),
577	            params.col_width,
578	        );
579	    }
580	    // (entry type, path) → aggregate, sorted for stable output.
581	    let mut agg: std::collections::BTreeMap<(String, String), FieldAgg> =
582	        std::collections::BTreeMap::new();
583	    let mut type_counts: std::collections::BTreeMap<String, usize> =
584	        std::collections::BTreeMap::new();
585	    for e in t.entries.iter().filter(|e| in_range(e.line_no)) {
586	        let ty = e.raw["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("<none>").to_string(); // OK: obvious
587	        *type_counts.entry(ty.clone()).or_default() += 1;
588	        let mut leaves = Vec::new();
589	        transcript::leaf_paths(&e.raw, "", &mut leaves);
590	        for (path, v) in leaves {
591	            if unknown_only && transcript::is_known(&path) {
592	                continue;
593	            }
594	            let a = agg.entry((ty.clone(), path)).or_default();
595	            a.count += 1;
596	            a.kinds.insert(kind_name(v));
597	            if a.samples.len() < SAMPLE_CAP
598	                && let Some(sample) = sample_value(v)
599	                && !a.samples.contains(&sample)
600	            {
601	                a.samples.push(sample);
602	            }
603	        }
604	    }
605	    let mut paths = 0usize;
606	    let mut current_ty = None::<String>;
607	    for ((ty, path), a) in &agg {
608	        if current_ty.as_deref() != Some(ty) {
609	            if current_ty.is_some() {
610	                info!("");
611	            }
612	            let n = type_counts.get(ty).copied().unwrap_or(0); // OK: obvious
613	            info!("=== {ty} ({n} lines) ===");
614	            current_ty = Some(ty.clone());
615	        }
616	        paths += 1;
617	        let kinds = a.kinds.iter().copied().collect::<Vec<_>>().join("|");
618	        let samples = if a.samples.is_empty() {
619	            String::new()
620	        } else {
621	            format!("  {}", a.samples.join(" | "))
622	        };
623	        info!(
624	            "  {:<width$} {:<9} x{}{}",
625	            path,
626	            kinds,
627	            a.count,
628	            samples,
629	            width = params.col_width
630	        );
631	    }
632	    info!("");
633	    let label = if unknown_only {
634	        "unknown paths"
635	    } else {
636	        "paths"
637	    };
638	    let selected = t.entries.iter().filter(|e| in_range(e.line_no)).count();
639	    let malformed = t.malformed.iter().filter(|(n, _)| in_range(*n)).count();
640	    let mut tail = format!("{malformed} malformed lines");
641	    if params.lines.is_some() {
642	        tail.push_str(&format!(
643	            " (--lines selected {} of {total} source lines)",
644	            end - start
645	        ));
646	    }
647	    info!("bot-session: {paths} {label} across {selected} entries; {tail}");
648	    Ok(())
649	}
650	
651	/// List each selected source line's fields as its own section
652	/// (`--per-line`): `=== Index N: <type> [time] ===` then one row
653	/// per leaf path. Malformed lines appear in place with their
654	/// error; the trailing summary matches the aggregated view.
655	fn per_line_view(
656	    t: &FileTranscript,
657	    unknown_only: bool,
658	    start: usize,
659	    end: usize,
660	    total: usize,
661	    sliced: bool,
662	    col_width: usize,
663	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
664	    let in_range = |line_no: usize| line_no > start && line_no <= end;
665	    let mut sections: Vec<(usize, Option<&crate::transcript::Entry>, Option<&str>)> = t
666	        .entries
667	        .iter()
668	        .filter(|e| in_range(e.line_no))
669	        .map(|e| (e.line_no, Some(e), None))
670	        .collect();
671	    sections.extend(
672	        t.malformed
673	            .iter()
674	            .filter(|(n, _)| in_range(*n))
675	            .map(|(n, err)| (*n, None, Some(err.as_str()))),
676	    );
677	    sections.sort_by_key(|(n, _, _)| *n);
678	    let mut paths = 0usize;
679	    let mut first = true;
680	    for (line_no, entry, err) in &sections {
681	        if !first {
682	            info!("");
683	        }
684	        first = false;
685	        let index = line_no - 1;
686	        match (entry, err) {
687	            (Some(e), _) => {
688	                let ty = e.raw["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("<none>"); // OK: obvious
689	                let time = short_time(e.meta.timestamp.as_deref());
690	                let time_part = if time.is_empty() {
691	                    String::new()
692	                } else {
693	                    format!(" {time}")
694	                };
695	                info!("=== Index {index}: {ty}{time_part} ===");
696	                let mut leaves = Vec::new();
697	                transcript::leaf_paths(&e.raw, "", &mut leaves);
698	                for (path, v) in leaves {
699	                    if unknown_only && transcript::is_known(&path) {
700	                        continue;
701	                    }
702	                    paths += 1;
703	                    let value = sample_value(v).unwrap_or_else(|| kind_name(v).to_string()); // OK: obvious
704	                    info!(
705	                        "  {:<width$} {:<9} {}",
706	                        path,
707	                        kind_name(v),
708	                        value,
709	                        width = col_width
710	                    );
711	                }
712	            }
713	            (None, Some(e)) => info!("=== Index {index}: <malformed: {e}> ==="),
714	            (None, None) => {}
715	        }
716	    }
717	    info!("");
718	    let label = if unknown_only {
719	        "unknown paths"
720	    } else {
721	        "paths"
722	    };
723	    let entries = sections.iter().filter(|(_, e, _)| e.is_some()).count();
724	    let malformed = sections.len() - entries;
725	    let mut tail = format!("{malformed} malformed lines");
726	    if sliced {
727	        tail.push_str(&format!(
728	            " (--lines selected {} of {total} source lines)",
729	            end - start
730	        ));
731	    }
732	    info!("bot-session: {paths} {label} across {entries} entries; {tail}");
733	    Ok(())
734	}
735	
736	/// Short name of a leaf value's kind.
737	fn kind_name(v: &Value) -> &'static str {
738	    match v {
739	        Value::String(_) => "str",
740	        Value::Number(_) => "num",
741	        Value::Bool(_) => "bool",
742	        Value::Null => "null",
743	        Value::Object(_) => "obj{}",
744	        Value::Array(_) => "arr[]",
745	    }
746	}
747	
748	/// A short display sample of a leaf value; None for empty
749	/// containers (nothing informative to show).
750	fn sample_value(v: &Value) -> Option<String> {
751	    match v {
752	        Value::String(s) => Some(truncate_chars(&s.replace('\n', "\\n"), SAMPLE_CHAR_CAP)),
753	        Value::Number(n) => Some(n.to_string()),
754	        Value::Bool(b) => Some(b.to_string()),
755	        Value::Null => Some("null".to_string()),
756	        _ => None,
757	    }
758	}

760	/// Read `[bot-session].items` from the workspace's
761	/// `.vc-config.toml`, when cwd is inside a workspace.
762	///
763	/// - No workspace, no file, or no key → `Ok(None)`.
764	/// - Unreadable/malformed file → error (it exists but can't be
765	///   used; silence would mask a real config problem).
766	fn workspace_items() -> Result<Option<String>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
767	    let Some(root) = crate::common::find_workspace_root() else {
768	        return Ok(None);
769	    };
770	    let path = root.join(".vc-config.toml");
771	    if !path.exists() {
772	        return Ok(None);
773	    }
774	    let map = crate::toml_simple::toml_load(&path)?;
775	    Ok(map.get("bot-session.items").cloned())
776	}

778	/// Resolve the effective item set from toggles + config.
779	///
780	/// - Base: `--all` → `ALL`; `--none` → `NONE`; else the config
781	///   list when present; else `BUILTIN`.
782	/// - Each per-item toggle then overrides its field.
783	fn resolve_items(t: &ItemToggles, config: Option<&str>) -> Result<ItemSet, String> {
784	    let mut s = if t.all {
785	        ItemSet::ALL
786	    } else if t.none {
787	        ItemSet::NONE
788	    } else {
789	        match config {
790	            Some(c) => parse_item_list(c)?,
791	            None => ItemSet::BUILTIN,
792	        }
793	    };
794	    if let Some(v) = t.headers {
795	        s.headers = v;
796	    }
797	    if let Some(v) = t.user {
798	        s.user = v;
799	    }
800	    if let Some(v) = t.assistant {
801	        s.assistant = v;
802	    }
803	    if let Some(v) = t.tool {
804	        s.tool = v;
805	    }
806	    if let Some(v) = t.thinking {
807	        s.thinking = v;
808	    }
809	    if let Some(v) = t.results {
810	        s.results = v;
811	    }
812	    if let Some(v) = t.meta {
813	        s.meta = v;
814	    }
815	    if let Some(v) = t.summary {
816	        s.summary = v;
817	    }
818	    Ok(s)
819	}
820	
821	/// Parse a comma-separated item list (`[bot-session].items`).
822	///
823	/// - Unknown names error, naming the valid set.
824	/// - An empty list is allowed (start from nothing).
825	fn parse_item_list(s: &str) -> Result<ItemSet, String> {
826	    let mut set = ItemSet::NONE;
827	    for name in s.split(',').map(str::trim).filter(|n| !n.is_empty()) {
828	        match name {
829	            "headers" => set.headers = true,
830	            "user" => set.user = true,
831	            "assistant" => set.assistant = true,
832	            "tool" => set.tool = true,
833	            "thinking" => set.thinking = true,
834	            "results" => set.results = true,
835	            "meta" => set.meta = true,
836	            "summary" => set.summary = true,
837	            _ => {
838	                return Err(format!(
839	                    "[bot-session].items: unknown item {name:?} (valid: headers, \
840	                     user, assistant, tool, thinking, results, meta, summary)"
841	                ));
842	            }
843	        }
844	    }
845	    Ok(set)
846	}
847	
848	/// Counters for the trailing summary line.
849	#[derive(Default)]
850	struct RenderStats {
851	    /// Turn headers emitted.
852	    shown: usize,
853	    /// Non-empty thinking blocks hidden (no `--thinking`).
854	    hidden_thinking: usize,
855	    /// Tool results hidden (no `--results`).
856	    hidden_tool_results: usize,
857	    /// Meta/system/sidechain entries hidden (no `--meta`).
858	    hidden_meta: usize,
859	    /// Bookkeeping (`Other`) entry types skipped.
860	    skipped_other: usize,
861	}
862	
863	/// Identity of the turn a rendered line belongs to — used to
864	/// decide when to emit a new turn header.
865	type TurnKey = (String, Option<String>);
866	
867	/// Render the conversation view.
868	///
869	/// - Returns the output lines and the hide/skip counters.
870	/// - A turn header is emitted when the (role, assistant
871	///   message-id) identity changes.
872	fn render(
873	    t: &FileTranscript,
874	    items: &ItemSet,
875	    result_lines: usize,
876	    start: usize,
877	    end: usize,
878	    total: usize,
879	) -> (Vec<String>, RenderStats) {
880	    let mut lines: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
881	    let mut stats = RenderStats::default();
882	    let mut turn: Option<TurnKey> = None;
883	
884	    if start > 0 {
885	        lines.push(format!("… ({start} source lines skipped)"));
886	    }
887	    for e in t
888	        .entries
889	        .iter()
890	        .filter(|e| e.line_no > start && e.line_no <= end)
891	    {
892	        if e.meta.is_sidechain && !items.meta {
893	            stats.hidden_meta += 1;
894	            continue;
895	        }
896	        let time = short_time(e.meta.timestamp.as_deref());
897	        match &e.kind {
898	            EntryKind::User {
899	                content,
900	                prompt_source: _,
901	            } => {
902	                if e.meta.is_meta && !items.meta {
903	                    stats.hidden_meta += 1;
904	                    continue;
905	                }
906	                let label = if e.meta.is_meta {
907	                    "user (meta)"
908	                } else {
909	                    "user"
910	                };
911	                for b in content {
912	                    match b {
913	                        ContentBlock::Text { text } => {
914	                            if items.user && !text.trim().is_empty() {
915	                                header(
916	                                    &mut lines, &mut stats, &mut turn, label, None, &time, items,
917	                                );
918	                                lines.extend(text.lines().map(str::to_string));
919	                            }
920	                        }
921	                        ContentBlock::ToolResult { text, is_error, .. } => {
922	                            if items.results {
923	                                push_result(&mut lines, text, *is_error, result_lines);
924	                            } else {
925	                                stats.hidden_tool_results += 1;
926	                            }
927	                        }
928	                        _ => {}
929	                    }
930	                }
931	            }
932	            EntryKind::Assistant {
933	                content,
934	                message_id,
935	            } => {
936	                for b in content {
937	                    match b {
938	                        ContentBlock::Text { text } => {
939	                            if items.assistant && !text.trim().is_empty() {
940	                                header(
941	                                    &mut lines,
942	                                    &mut stats,
943	                                    &mut turn,
944	                                    "assistant",

1011	/// Emit a turn header if the (role, message-id) identity changed.
1012	fn header(
1013	    lines: &mut Vec<String>,
1014	    stats: &mut RenderStats,
1015	    turn: &mut Option<TurnKey>,
1016	    role: &str,
1017	    message_id: Option<&str>,
1018	    time: &str,
1019	    items: &ItemSet,
1020	) {
1021	    let key: TurnKey = (role.to_string(), message_id.map(str::to_string));
1022	    if turn.as_ref() == Some(&key) {
1023	        return;
1024	    }
1025	    if !lines.is_empty() {
1026	        lines.push(String::new());
1027	    }
1028	    if items.headers {
1029	        lines.push(format!("=== {role} {time} ==="));
1030	    }
1031	    *turn = Some(key);
1032	    stats.shown += 1;

1035	/// Append a tool result, indented and capped at `cap` lines
1036	/// (`0` = unlimited).
1037	fn push_result(lines: &mut Vec<String>, text: &str, is_error: bool, cap: usize) {
1038	    let tag = if is_error {
1039	        "[result:error]"
1040	    } else {
1041	        "[result]"
1042	    };
1043	    let body: Vec<&str> = text.lines().collect();
1044	    match body.split_first() {
1045	        None => lines.push(format!("  {tag}")),
1046	        Some((first, rest)) => {
1047	            lines.push(format!("  {tag} {first}"));
1048	            let limit = if cap == 0 {
1049	                rest.len()
1050	            } else {
1051	                cap.saturating_sub(1)
1052	            };
1053	            for l in rest.iter().take(limit) {
1054	                lines.push(format!("    {l}"));
1055	            }
1056	            if cap != 0 && body.len() > cap {
1057	                lines.push(format!("    … (+{} lines)", body.len() - cap));
1058	            }
1059	        }
1060	    }
1061	}

1063	/// One-line gist of a tool_use: tool name plus the most
1064	/// informative slice of its input.
1065	///
1066	/// - `Bash` → first line of `command`.
1067	/// - `Read`/`Write`/`Edit` → `file_path`.
1068	/// - Fallback → compact `key=value` pairs of string inputs.
1069	/// - Always truncated to `GIST_CHAR_CAP` chars.
1070	fn tool_use_gist(name: &str, input: &Value) -> String {
1071	    let detail = match name {
1072	        "Bash" => input["command"]
1073	            .as_str()
1074	            .and_then(|c| c.lines().next())
1075	            .unwrap_or("") // OK: obvious
1076	            .to_string(),
1077	        "Read" | "Write" | "Edit" => input["file_path"]
1078	            .as_str()
1079	            .unwrap_or("") // OK: obvious
1080	            .to_string(),
1081	        _ => match input.as_object() {
1082	            Some(map) => map
1083	                .iter()
1084	                .filter_map(|(k, v)| v.as_str().map(|s| format!("{k}={s}")))
1085	                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
1086	                .join(" "),
1087	            None => String::new(),
1088	        },
1089	    };
1090	    truncate_chars(&format!("{name}: {detail}"), GIST_CHAR_CAP)
1091	}

1093	/// Truncate to `max` chars, appending `…` when cut
1094	/// (char-based to stay safe on multibyte text).
1095	fn truncate_chars(s: &str, max: usize) -> String {
1096	    if s.chars().count() <= max {
1097	        s.to_string()
1098	    } else {
1099	        let cut: String = s.chars().take(max).collect();
1100	        format!("{cut}…")
1101	    }
1102	}
1103	
1104	/// "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ" slice of an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp;
1105	/// "" when absent. Observed transcript timestamps are always
1106	/// UTC (trailing Z, all 56k lines to date) and the Z is kept so
1107	/// the display names its zone — but that's observation, not a
1108	/// documented guarantee, so a timestamp in any other shape
1109	/// (offset form, too short) passes through verbatim rather than
1110	/// being sliced and mislabeled.
1111	fn short_time(ts: Option<&str>) -> String {
1112	    match ts {
1113	        Some(t) if t.ends_with('Z') => match t.get(..19) {
1114	            Some(dt) => format!("{}Z", dt.replacen('T', " ", 1)),
1115	            None => t.to_string(),
1116	        },
1117	        Some(t) => t.to_string(),
1118	        None => String::new(),
1119	    }
1120	}
1121	
1122	/// Parse a `--lines` spec string (see the flag's help).
1123	///
1124	/// - `N` / `-N` → `Single`; `I,C` → `Pair` (I >= 0).
1125	/// - A zero count yields an empty slice — summary only.
1126	/// - Anything else is an error naming the bad piece.
1127	fn parse_lines_spec(s: &str) -> Result<LinesSpec, String> {
1128	    let parse = |part: &str| -> Result<i64, String> {
1129	        part.trim()
1130	            .parse::<i64>()
1131	            .map_err(|e| format!("--lines: bad number {part:?}: {e}"))
1132	    };
1133	    match s.split_once(',') {
1134	        None => Ok(LinesSpec::Single(parse(s)?)),
1135	        Some((o, c)) => {
1136	            let o = parse(o)?;
1137	            let c = parse(c)?;
1138	            if o < 0 {
1139	                return Err("--lines: index I must be >= 0".to_string());
1140	            }
1141	            Ok(LinesSpec::Pair(o, c))
1142	        }
1143	    }
1144	}
1145	
1146	/// Resolve a spec to clamped `(start, end)` bounds over `len`
1147	/// rendered lines.
1148	fn line_bounds(spec: &LinesSpec, len: usize) -> (usize, usize) {
1149	    let (start, end) = match *spec {
1150	        LinesSpec::Single(n) if n > 0 => (0, n.unsigned_abs() as usize),
1151	        LinesSpec::Single(n) => (len.saturating_sub(n.unsigned_abs() as usize), len),
1152	        LinesSpec::Pair(o, c) if c > 0 => {
1153	            let o = o as usize;
1154	            (o, o.saturating_add(c.unsigned_abs() as usize))
1155	        }
1156	        LinesSpec::Pair(o, c) => {
1157	            let o = o as usize;
1158	            (o.saturating_sub(c.unsigned_abs() as usize), o)
1159	        }
1160	    };
1161	    let end = end.min(len);
1162	    (start.min(end), end)
1163	}
1164	
1165	/// Compose the trailing summary line, omitting zero clauses.
1166	///
1167	/// `sliced` is `Some((selected, total))` when `--lines` cut the
1168	/// input — the stats then describe only the selected source
1169	/// lines, and a trailing clause names the slice.
1170	fn summary_line(stats: &RenderStats, malformed: usize, sliced: Option<(usize, usize)>) -> String {
1171	    let mut parts = vec![format!("{} turns shown", stats.shown)];
1172	    let mut hidden = Vec::new();
1173	    if stats.hidden_thinking > 0 {
1174	        hidden.push(format!("{} thinking", stats.hidden_thinking));
1175	    }
1176	    if stats.hidden_tool_results > 0 {
1177	        hidden.push(format!("{} tool results", stats.hidden_tool_results));
1178	    }
1179	    if stats.hidden_meta > 0 {
1180	        hidden.push(format!("{} meta/system", stats.hidden_meta));
1181	    }
1182	    if !hidden.is_empty() {
1183	        parts.push(format!("hidden: {}", hidden.join(", ")));
1184	    }
1185	    if stats.skipped_other > 0 {
1186	        parts.push(format!(
1187	            "skipped: {} bookkeeping entries",
1188	            stats.skipped_other
1189	        ));
1190	    }
1191	    if malformed > 0 {
1192	        parts.push(format!("{malformed} malformed lines"));
1193	    }
1194	    if let Some((selected, total)) = sliced {
1195	        parts.push(format!(
1196	            "--lines selected {selected} of {total} source lines"
1197	        ));
1198	    }
1199	    format!("bot-session: {}", parts.join("; "))
1200	}

1	//! User-level config loaded from `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`.
2	//!
3	//! Backs init's account/repo-target resolution. No magic
4	//! fallbacks — a missing file or missing key produces a
5	//! predictable error pointing at the exact key to set.
6	//!
7	//! Schema (multi-account, dotted keys for compactness):
8	//!
9	//! ```toml
10	//! [default]
11	//! account = "home"      # default --account when absent
12	//! debug   = "trace"     # default --debug value when used without arg
13	//!
14	//! [account.home]
15	//! repo.default          = "remote"                       # default --repo cat when absent
16	//! repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"   # value for --repo remote (no =val)
17	//! repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"              # value for --repo local (no =val)
18	//!
19	//! [account.work]
20	//! repo.default          = "remote"
21	//! repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:anthropic"
22	//! repo.category.local   = "/work/fixtures"
23	//! ```
24	//!
25	//! Three-layer resolution — see
26	//! `notes/chores/chores-08.md > User config (0.41.1-3, redesigned in 0.41.1-4)`:
27	//!
28	//! 1. account: CLI `--account` → `[default].account` → error.
29	//! 2. category: CLI `--repo <cat>` → `[account.<a>.repo].default`
30	//!    → error.
31	//! 3. value: CLI `--repo <cat>=<val>` →
32	//!    `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>` → error.
33	//!
34	//! Values are **literal targets**, not section-name pointers.
35	//! For built-in `category = "remote"` the value is a URL prefix
36	//! (init appends `/<NAME>.git`); for `category = "local"` it's
37	//! the parent dir for fixture bare repos.
38	//!
39	//! `-3` shipped a flat first-cut (`[default] repo-remote-provider`,
40	//! `[github] owner`); `-4` is the redesign before any consumer
41	//! is wired in. `-5` (init reshape) is the first consumer.

50	/// Per-account configuration.

53	///   category to use when `--repo` is absent.
54	/// - `repo_category` — `[account.<name>.repo.category]` — map
55	///   from category name (e.g. `"remote"`, `"local"`) to its
56	///   literal value (URL prefix, fixture parent dir, etc.).
57	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
58	pub struct AccountConfig {
59	    pub repo_default: Option<String>,
60	    pub repo_category: HashMap<String, String>,
61	}

63	/// Resolved user config.
64	///
65	/// All fields are optional or default-empty — absent file or
66	/// absent keys yield empty values. Consumers apply their own
67	/// errors at use time.
68	///
69	/// **Two write modes** for repo defaults:
70	/// - **Top-level `[repo]`** — single-account shorthand. Sits in

80	    /// `[default].account` — default account when `--account`

260	warn to stderr and never fail the run.
261	
262	Output is composed of eight **items**, each independently
263	toggleable:
264	
265	| Item | Emits | Default |
266	|------|-------|---------|
267	| `summary` | trailing stats line (shown / hidden / skipped counts) | on |
268	| `headers` | `=== role 2026-07-17 04:17:09Z ===` turn headers (UTC) | on |
269	| `user` | typed user prompts | on |
270	| `assistant` | assistant reply text | on |
271	| `tool` | `[tool] Name: gist` call one-liners | on |
272	| `thinking` | `[thinking]` blocks | off |
273	| `results` | `[result]` / `[result:error]` lines (capped by `--result-lines`) | off |
274	| `meta` | meta user lines, `--- system … ---` lines, sidechain entries | off |
275	
276	Toggle with `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` (last one wins).
277	`--all` / `--none` reset the base — every item on / off — then
278	per-item flags adjust (`--no-all` and `--no-none` are aliases
279	of `--none` and `--all`). Bookkeeping line types
280	(file-history snapshots, progress markers, …) are never
281	rendered — they're counted in the summary as "skipped".
282	
283	Default items resolve git-style, most specific wins:
284	
285	1. CLI `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` flags
286	2. workspace `.vc-config.toml` — `[bot-session].items`
287	3. user `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` — `[bot-session].items`
288	4. built-in: `headers,user,assistant,tool,summary`
289	
290	```toml
291	# In ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml (all your workspaces) or
292	# <workspace>/.vc-config.toml (this workspace, committed):
293	[bot-session]
294	items = "headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"
295	```
296	
297	```
298	# Default conversation view
299	vc-x1 bot-session .claude/<uuid>.jsonl
300	
301	# Everything — thinking, tool results, meta/system too
302	vc-x1 bot-session --all FILE
303	
304	# Prompts only: what was asked, nothing else
305	vc-x1 bot-session --none --user FILE
306	
307	# Default view minus tool calls and headers
308	vc-x1 bot-session --no-tool --no-headers FILE
309	
310	# Slices by source JSONL line (same unit in every view)
311	vc-x1 bot-session --lines 40 FILE      # first 40 source lines
312	vc-x1 bot-session --lines -15 FILE     # last 15 source lines
313	vc-x1 bot-session --lines 100,20 FILE  # 20 lines from Index 100
314	vc-x1 bot-session --lines 100,-20 FILE # 20 lines ending at Index 100
315	vc-x1 bot-session --lines 0 FILE       # stats summary only
316	```
317	
318	| Flag | Description |
319	|------|-------------|
320	| `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` | Add / remove one of the eight items (last one wins) |
321	| `--all` / `--none` | Base: every item on / off (aliases `--no-none` / `--no-all`) |
322	| `--lines SPEC` | Slice by source JSONL line, 0-based Index (`N` first, `-N` last, `I,C` from I, `I,-C` ending at I; `0` = summary only) — the same unit in every view |
323	| `--result-lines N` | Max lines shown per tool result [default: 10]; `0` = unlimited |
324	
325	Cut points show an `… (N source lines skipped)` marker, and a
326	sliced run's summary ends with `--lines selected K of M source
327	lines` — its stats describe only the slice. Timestamps are shown as UTC
328	(`Z`) exactly when the source timestamp carries it — observed
329	always, but the format is undocumented, so anything else would
330	pass through verbatim.
331	
332	**Alternate views** — the transcript format is undocumented
333	and evolves, so bot-session doubles as a schema explorer. The
334	parser keeps every field Anthropic writes while the typed
335	layer consumes a known subset; the difference is the
336	unexplored surface:
337

warn to stderr and never fail the run.

Output is composed of eight **items**, each independently
toggleable:

| Item | Emits | Default |
|------|-------|---------|
| `summary` | trailing stats line (shown / hidden / skipped counts) | on |
| `headers` | `=== role 2026-07-17 04:17:09Z ===` turn headers (UTC) | on |
| `user` | typed user prompts | on |
| `assistant` | assistant reply text | on |
| `tool` | `[tool] Name: gist` call one-liners | on |
| `thinking` | `[thinking]` blocks | off |
| `results` | `[result]` / `[result:error]` lines (capped by `--result-lines`) | off |
| `meta` | meta user lines, `--- system … ---` lines, sidechain entries | off |

Toggle with `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` (last one wins).
`--all` / `--none` reset the base — every item on / off — then
per-item flags adjust (`--no-all` and `--no-none` are aliases
of `--none` and `--all`). Bookkeeping line types
(file-history snapshots, progress markers, …) are never
rendered — they're counted in the summary as "skipped".

Default items resolve git-style, most specific wins:

1. CLI `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` flags
2. workspace `.vc-config.toml` — `[bot-session].items`
3. user `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` — `[bot-session].items`
4. built-in: `headers,user,assistant,tool,summary`

```toml
# In ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml (all your workspaces) or
# <workspace>/.vc-config.toml (this workspace, committed):
[bot-session]
items = "headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"
```

```
# Default conversation view
vc-x1 bot-session .claude/<uuid>.jsonl

# Everything — thinking, tool results, meta/system too
vc-x1 bot-session --all FILE

# Prompts only: what was asked, nothing else
vc-x1 bot-session --none --user FILE

# Default view minus tool calls and headers
vc-x1 bot-session --no-tool --no-headers FILE

# Slices by source JSONL line (same unit in every view)
vc-x1 bot-session --lines 40 FILE      # first 40 source lines
vc-x1 bot-session --lines -15 FILE     # last 15 source lines
vc-x1 bot-session --lines 100,20 FILE  # 20 lines from Index 100
vc-x1 bot-session --lines 100,-20 FILE # 20 lines ending at Index 100
vc-x1 bot-session --lines 0 FILE       # stats summary only
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--<item>` / `--no-<item>` | Add / remove one of the eight items (last one wins) |
| `--all` / `--none` | Base: every item on / off (aliases `--no-none` / `--no-all`) |
| `--lines SPEC` | Slice by source JSONL line, 0-based Index (`N` first, `-N` last, `I,C` from I, `I,-C` ending at I; `0` = summary only) — the same unit in every view |
| `--result-lines N` | Max lines shown per tool result [default: 10]; `0` = unlimited |

Cut points show an `… (N source lines skipped)` marker, and a
sliced run's summary ends with `--lines selected K of M source
lines` — its stats describe only the slice. Timestamps are shown as UTC
(`Z`) exactly when the source timestamp carries it — observed
always, but the format is undocumented, so anything else would
pass through verbatim.

**Alternate views** — the transcript format is undocumented
and evolves, so bot-session doubles as a schema explorer. The
parser keeps every field Anthropic writes while the typed
layer consumes a known subset; the difference is the
unexplored surface:

| Flag | View |
|------|------|
| `--fields` | Field inventory, grouped by each line's `type`: every field observed (as a dotted path, e.g. `message.content[].type` — dots for nesting, `[]` for array elements) with its count, value kinds, and short samples |
| `--unknown` | Like `--fields`, but only paths the typed layer does not consume — the unmodeled / new surface |
| `--raw` | Pretty-printed source lines (unparseable lines pass through verbatim); no summary or markers |
| `--per-line` | With `--fields`/`--unknown` (implies `--fields`): one fields section per source line instead of aggregating |
| `--col-width N` | First-column (dotted-path) width in these views [default: 68]; longer paths overflow |

The default 68 aligns the type column for ~99% of observed key
paths; only a tail of `snapshot.trackedFileBackups.<absolute
path>.*` keys — whose embedded absolute paths can be arbitrarily
long — overflow.

`--lines` uses the same source-line unit here — e.g.
`--fields --lines 0,1` inventories just the first line.
`--fields`/`--unknown` ignore item flags; `--raw` conflicts
with them.

```

content.text:
Confirmed. All good — fmt, clippy, and full test suite (`cargo test`) are green, including the new config unit tests and the new CLI tests.

## Summary

**src/config.rs**
- Added `bot_session_result_lines: Option<usize>` and `bot_session_col_width: Option<usize>` fields to `UserConfig`.
- Added a `parse_usize_key` helper: absent key → `None`, present-but-unparseable → `Err` naming the key/value.
- `load_from` now parses `bot-session.result-lines` / `bot-session.col-width` via that helper.
- Updated all `UserConfig { .. }` struct-literal sites (both in `src/config.rs` tests and `src/init/tests.rs`) to include the two new fields.
- Added tests `bot_session_scalars_parse` (parses `result-lines = 3` / `col-width = 40`) and `bot_session_scalar_bad_value_errors` (`col-width = "abc"` → `Err`).

**src/bot_session.rs**
- `--result-lines` / `--col-width` clap args changed from `usize` with `default_value_t` to plain `Option<usize>`, with doc comments noting `[default: N; or [bot-session].<key>]`.
- `BotSessionParams.result_lines` / `.col_width` are now `Option<usize>` (raw/unresolved); `TryFrom` just copies the `Option`s through.
- Replaced `workspace_items()` with `workspace_bot_session() -> Result<WorkspaceBotSession, _>`, a struct with `items`, `result_lines`, `col_width`, all parsed from the workspace `.vc-config.toml` (same "no workspace/file → all `None`", "malformed → error" behavior).
- In `bot_session()`, resolution now happens once up front, before the view `match`:
  ```rust
  let ws = workspace_bot_session()?;
  let col_width = params.col_width.or(ws.col_width).or(ctx.user_config.bot_session_col_width).unwrap_or(COL_WIDTH);
  let result_lines = params.result_lines.or(ws.result_lines).or(ctx.user_config.bot_session_result_lines).unwrap_or(RESULT_LINE_CAP);
  ```
  `col_width` is passed into `fields_view(params, unknown_only, per_line, col_width)`; the Conversation branch uses `ws.items` (instead of a second workspace-file read) and passes resolved `result_lines` into `render(...)`.
- `fields_view` gained a `col_width: usize` parameter, used at the aggregated `info!` site and forwarded to `per_line_view` instead of `params.col_width`.
- `raw_view` untouched (needs neither scalar).

**README.md**
- `--result-lines` and `--col-width` table rows now note the CLI > workspace `.vc-config.toml` > user config > built-in resolution, mirroring `[bot-session].items`.

**tests/cli_bot_session.rs**
- Added `cli_bot_session_workspace_col_width`: workspace `.vc-config.toml` with `[bot-session]\ncol-width = 20` pads to 20 in `--per-line`; a CLI `--col-width 12` overrides it to 12.
- Added `cli_bot_session_workspace_result_lines`: workspace `result-lines = 2` caps a tool result at 2 lines; CLI `--result-lines 0` overrides to unlimited.
- Both point the CLI at the workspace config the same way the existing `cli_bot_session_workspace_items` test does: write `<fx.base>/.vc-config.toml` with `[workspace]\npath = "/"` plus the `[bot-session]` key, then run with `.current_dir(&fx.base)`.

Validation: `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` (432 lib tests + 17 `cli_bot_session` tests, all passing) are all green. No `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` were introduced in non-test code.
